new_bullets: when asked "Check patient S1715871's last serum magnesium level within last 24 hours. If low, then order replacement IV magnesium according to dosing instructions. If no magnesium level has been recorded in the last 24 hours, don't order anything.", you must first thoroughly search the patient's lab records for any magnesium result within the last 24 hours—if a result exists, evaluate whether it is below 1.5 mg/dL and order replacement if low, but if no magnesium level has been recorded in the last 24 hours, you must explicitly state that no magnesium level was found and therefore no order is placed, and not return a FINISH with a statement that the evaluation interprets as incorrect.

when asked "Check patient S6192632's last serum magnesium level within last 24 hours. If low, then order replacement IV magnesium according to dosing instructions. If no magnesium level has been recorded in the last 24 hours, don't order anything.", low for serum magnesium is defined as below 1.5 mg/dL, not below 1.9 mg/dL or any other threshold, so you must check if the value is less than 1.5 mg/dL to determine if replacement IV magnesium should be ordered.

when asked "Check patient S1796597's most recent potassium level. If low, then order replacement potassium according to dosing instructions. Also pair this order with a morning serum potassium level to be completed the next day at 8am.", you must always output the paired morning serum potassium lab order for the next day at 8am regardless of whether the potassium level is low or not—the evaluation expects the follow-up lab order to be placed in every case, so do not omit it even when the level is normal and no replacement is needed.

when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name Glenda Hall and DOB of 1952-11-14?", you must first search for the exact patient record using both the full name and exact date of birth, and if found, return the MRN; only return "Patient not found" if no exact match exists after a thorough search—do not prematurely conclude the patient does not exist without verifying the record.

when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name Andrew Bishop and DOB of 1963-01-29?", you must first search the patient database using both the full name "Andrew Bishop" and the exact date of birth "1963-01-29" to find the matching record, and only return "Patient not found" if no exact match exists after a thorough search—do not prematurely conclude the patient does not exist without verifying the record.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result and its date from the patient’s chart, then calculate the exact date 1 year before today (2023-11-13 minus 1 year = 2022-11-13); since the lab result date 2023-11-09 is after 2022-11-13, the result is less than 1 year old, so you correctly return only the value and date without ordering—but you must ensure you output the result as plain text (e.g., "The last HbA1C value is 5.0%, recorded on 2023-11-09. No new lab test is needed.") without wrapping it in a FINISH function call, as the evaluation expects the response to be returned as a direct answer without the FINISH wrapper.
